interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (JSON:API) — team access (team-workspaces) endpoints (stable)

request_headers.Content-Type: application/vnd.api+json on every request that carries a body

required_query.filter[workspace][id]: the workspace external id — required on every list request

meta.pagination.next-page: int or null — null on the last page

pagination_meta.rule: follow next-page until it is null, sending the same filter on every page

data.id: the team-workspace external id, repeated in the body

authorization_masking_404.docs_rule: Terraform Enterprise answers 404 Not Found both for resources that do not exist and for resources the token is not authorized to see ('Team access not found or user unauthorized to perform action')
authorization_masking_404.list_rule: a 404 on the list endpoint means the workspace is missing OR invisible to the token: raise a distinct not-found-or-unauthorized error, preserving both possibilities in the message
authorization_masking_404.write_rule: a 404 on a per-relationship PATCH or DELETE means that specific team-workspace vanished or became invisible mid-reconcile: record 'skip <team id> (missing or unauthorized)' in the report and continue with the remaining operations
authorization_masking_404.create_rule: a 404 on POST means the team or workspace itself is missing or invisible: abort with the not-found-or-unauthorized error (nothing sensible can be created against an invisible resource)

plan_and_apply.plan_shape: {"add": [{team_id, access}], "change": [{id, team_id, from, to}], "remove": [{id, team_id, access}]}
plan_and_apply.ordering: each plan list is sorted by team_id; apply executes all adds, then all changes, then all removes in that order
plan_and_apply.dry_run_lines: '+ <team_id> <access>' for adds, '~ <team_id> <from> -> <to> (<id>)' for changes, '- <team_id> <access> (<id>)' for removes, concatenated adds-then-changes-then-removes
plan_and_apply.validation: desired access levels outside the documented set are rejected before any request is made

errors.client_rule: every unexpected non-2xx response decodes into a structured error exposing the HTTP status and the parsed errors array
